question_text: Let (𝑋1, 𝑌1), (𝑋2, 𝑌2), … , (𝑋𝑛, 𝑌𝑛), 𝑛≥2, be a random sample from a continuous

options.B: ivariate distribution with joint distribution function 𝐹𝑋,𝑌. Further, 𝐹𝑋 and 𝐹𝑌 are the marginal distribution functions of 𝑋 and 𝑌, respectively. If 𝐹𝑋,𝑌(𝑥, 𝑦) = 𝐹𝑋(𝑥)𝐹𝑌(𝑦), ∀ (𝑥, 𝑦), then, for any two independent pairs (𝑋𝑖, 𝑌𝑖) and (𝑋𝑗, 𝑌𝑗), 𝑃[(𝑋𝑖−𝑋𝑗)(𝑌𝑖−𝑌𝑗) > 0] equals
options.C: (D) Q.23 For a random sample 𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑛, 𝑛≥2, from a population with distribution function 𝐹𝑋, let 𝑍𝑛(𝑥) be the proportion of sample values less than or equal to 𝑥, 𝑥∈ℝ. Which of the following statements is/are true?
options.D: 𝑍𝑛(𝑥) is a consistent estimator of 𝐹𝑋(𝑥)
